research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided Excel file, reproduce the line plot corresponding to the spatially averaged tunneling differential conductance spectrum on the Sb-terminated surface. Read the tabular data, identify the bias-voltage axis and the dI/dV signal, and generate a publication-style plot with bias on the x-axis and normalized conductance on the y-axis. Preserve the original curve shape, axis scaling, and any annotations that are encoded in the spreadsheet. If the sheet contains multiple traces or repeated headers, parse them carefully and plot only the spectrum data intended for the figure. The goal is a clean replot of the figure panel, not a new analysis.
execution_steps: Step_0: Open the Excel workbook and identify the sheet corresponding to the dI/dV spectrum.
Step_1: Parse the bias-voltage column and the dI/dV intensity column, handling any repeated headers or formatting artifacts.
Step_2: Clean the data into a single ordered x-y series suitable for plotting.
Step_3: Create a line plot of dI/dV versus bias with publication-style formatting and labeled axes.
Step_4: Verify that the plotted curve is smooth and matches the expected spectral profile from the digitized data.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A replot of the Sb-terminated tunneling spectrum as a single dI/dV-versus-bias curve, suitable for direct comparison with the corresponding paper panel.

research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided Excel file, reproduce the inset spectrum panel associated with the tunneling differential conductance data. Inspect the sheet structure, identify the bias-voltage axis and the dI/dV signal, and generate a clean line plot from the digitized data. If the sheet contains a zoomed-in bias window or a second trace intended for an inset, preserve that view and plot range. The objective is to recreate the inset figure panel faithfully from the spreadsheet data, using standard scientific plotting conventions.
execution_steps: Step_0: Open the Excel workbook and locate the sheet corresponding to the inset spectrum.
Step_1: Determine which columns contain bias and dI/dV values, accounting for any multi-row headers or embedded labels.
Step_2: Extract the intended inset-range data and remove any non-data rows.
Step_3: Plot dI/dV versus bias using a line plot with clear axis labels and a zoomed-in view if indicated by the data.
Step_4: Confirm that the output matches the inset-style presentation implied by the digitized dataset.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A clean replot of the inset tunneling spectrum panel, showing the digitized dI/dV signal over the appropriate bias range.
